paper_title: Effects of Titanium Dioxide nanoparticles on salinity tolerance of rice (Oryza sativa L.) at the seedling stage

entities.description: The study of the functioning and processes of plants, specifically regarding abiotic stress.
The ability of rice to grow and develop in high-salt soil conditions.
A process caused by the accumulation of reactive oxygen species (ROS) in plants.
Nanoparticles (TiO2) used as a chemical priming agent to enhance plant stress resistance.
Sodium chloride used to induce salt stress conditions in the experiment.
Method used to estimate chlorophyll, carotenoids, and proline contents.
Software used for statistical analysis of experimental data.
The most effective concentration of TiO2 for promoting rice growth under stress.
The percentage increase in shoot height of ST25 rice variety.
The improvement in survival rate of ST25 rice variety compared to control.
The increase in Peroxidase enzyme activity at the third spray.
Corresponding author and third author of the study.
